{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question is whether secondary glioblastoma can be caused by brain irradiation. Reviewing the documents, Document [19] (Title: Optic Nerve Glioma -- Radiation Oncology -- Secondary Malignancies) explicitly states that 'the development of a secondary CNS tumor usually occurs within the high-dose radiation field with a mean time to development of 6 to 9 years and a 10-year cumulative incidence of 8%. The risk is particularly high in patients with NF1, with 50% of the irradiated patients developing a second CNS tumor. These secondary tumors can include anaplastic astrocytoma and glioblastoma multiforme.' This directly indicates that brain irradiation can lead to the development of secondary glioblastoma multiforme. Therefore, based on the evidence in the retrieved documents, secondary glioblastoma can indeed be caused by brain irradiation.", "answer_choice": "A"}
